methodology: Cross-reference network analysis (33-vote connection Hb10:19→Hb13:6) + patristic commentary reading (Lapide quadruple hermeneutics, Ephrem, Chrysostom, Haydock) + CCEL major discovery (Bonar 'The Rent Veil' — 5096 lines dedicated to this theme) + OT typological chain (Lv 16 → Ex 26 → Hb 9 → Mt 27) + topic verification (ACCESS_TO_GOD, ATONEMENT, TABERNACLE)

evidence: commentaries (Lapide quadruple reading, Haydock, Hilary, Jerome — 5 entries)
commentaries (Chrysostom 'flesh = veil, first cut the way', Haydock)
commentaries (Ephrem 'Spirit tore the curtain', Gregory the Theologian)
commentaries (Chrysostom 'veil = type of heaven, through hope we are already in heaven')

relevance_change.reason: 19 votes to HEB.11.1 (faith/hope). 6 votes to LEV.16.2 and LEV.16.15 (Levitical foundation). Chrysostom: 'through hope we are already in heaven.' The verse that bridges the physical veil (OT) to the spiritual reality (hope penetrating beyond the veil).

new_refs_added.justification: 5 votes para JHN.14.6. Haydock cites Mt 27:51 DIRECTLY when commenting on this verse! Explains WHY the veil existed: 'the way of the Holy Place was not yet manifested while the first tabernacle was still standing.' Essential 'before' context for the tearing event.

9 votes de HEB.10.20. 15 votes para EPH.3.12. Practical result of the torn veil: 'Let us draw near with confidence to the throne of grace.' Topic ACCESS_TO_GOD. Completes the chain: prohibition (Lv 16) → tearing (Mt 27) → access (Hb 4:16).

candidates_noted.justification: 6 votes de HEB.6.19 and HEB.10.19. Haydock connects explicitly to Hb 10:19-20. OT prohibition foundation: 'do not enter the sanctuary at all times, inside the veil.'
6 votes de HEB.6.19. 5 votes de HEB.10.20 and EXO.26.33. Architectural description: 'behind the second veil, the Holy of Holies.' Context for what was torn.
5 votes de HEB.9.3. Material description of the veil. Colors (blue, purple, scarlet) carry typological significance (royalty, priesthood).

central_equation.description: Equation confirmed by multiple patristic commentators
identifications: Veil of the temple = Flesh/Body of Christ (Hb 10:20, confirmed by Chrysostom, Johnson)
Veil torn = Body of Christ torn on the cross (Mt 27:51 ↔ Hb 10:20)
Holy of Holies = Heaven (Chrysostom on Hb 6:19, Haydock on Lv 16:2)
Access to Holy of Holies = Access to God (Hb 10:19, topic ACCESS_TO_GOD)

lapide_quadruple_hermeneutic.source: Cornelius a Lapide, Commentary on Matthew 27:51
levels: 1. Literal: The veil tore from top to bottom — divine action, not human
2. Allegorical: The veil of legal ceremonies was opened, fulfilled in Christ
3. Moral: Knowledge of God/Christ/Mysteries now open to Jews AND Gentiles
4. Anagogical: The way to Heaven opened — Holy of Holies was type of Heaven, veil meant it was closed until Christ broke through by His death
lapide_quadruple_hermeneutic.significance: Classic patristic fourfold interpretation (Quadriga) applied to a single event — demonstrates the depth of meaning the Church Fathers saw in this moment

bonar_discovery.size: 5096 lines — entire monograph dedicated to this theme
bonar_discovery.key_quote: 'Through the veil, which is His flesh, is our one access to God; not merely at first when we believed, but day by day, to the last.'
bonar_discovery.significance: Validates that this theme carries sufficient theological weight for monographic treatment. Rare to find an entire CCEL work dedicated to a single concept in our query set.

chain: Lv 16:2 (prohibition: do not enter) → Hb 9:8 (way not yet manifested)
→ Mt 27:51 / Mk 15:38 / Lk 23:45 (veil torn — the EVENT)
→ Hb 10:19-20 (the THEOLOGY: new and living way through his flesh)
→ Hb 6:19 (hope as anchor beyond the veil)
→ Hb 4:16 (practical result: draw near with confidence)
